subject: ap-precalculus
title: Symbolic Manipulation: Logarithm Properties, Trig Identities & Inverse Functions

parts.prompt:
(i) Rewrite $\log_3(81x^2) - \log_3(9x)$ as a single logarithm or simplified expression. Show the properties of logarithms used.

(ii) Solve $2^{3x-1} = 5^x$ for $x$. Express your answer as an exact value.
parts.scoring_notes: For part (i), quotient rule then simplification: $\log_3(81x^2/9x) = \log_3(9x) = 2 + \log_3 x$. Either equivalent form is correct. Decimal approximations not accepted. For part (ii), take $\ln$ of both sides: $(3x-1)\ln 2 = x\ln 5$; collecting $x$: $x(3\ln 2 - \ln 5) = \ln 2$; $x = \frac{\ln 2}{3\ln 2 - \ln 5}$. Accept equivalent exact forms. Common error: forgetting to distribute $\ln 2$ across $(3x-1)$.

scoring_points.description: Correct simplification to $\log_3(9x)$ or $2 + \log_3 x$; supporting work must show the quotient rule and the evaluation of $\log_3(9) = 2$
common_traps: Quotient rule: $\log_3 A - \log_3 B = \log_3(A/B)$, not $\log_3(A - B)$
$\log_3 9 = 2$ since $3^2 = 9$ — this simplification step must appear

scoring_points.description: Correct exact solution $x = \frac{\ln 2}{3\ln 2 - \ln 5}$; accept equivalent forms such as $\frac{\ln 2}{\ln 8 - \ln 5} = \frac{\ln 2}{\ln(8/5)}$; supporting work must show taking $\ln$ of both sides and collecting $x$ terms
common_traps: $(3x-1)\ln 2 = 3x\ln 2 - \ln 2$, NOT $3x\ln 2 - 1$ — the $-1$ is an exponent on $2$, so it becomes $-\ln 2$ after taking the natural log

parts.prompt:
(i) Use a trigonometric identity to simplify $\frac{\sin^2(\theta)}{1 - \cos^2(\theta)} \cdot \tan(\theta)$. Show the identities used and simplify completely.

(ii) Find all solutions to $2\cos^2(\theta) - \cos(\theta) - 1 = 0$ on the interval $0 \leq \theta < 2\pi$. Express answers as exact values.
parts.scoring_notes: For part (i), the Pythagorean identity $1 - \cos^2\theta = \sin^2\theta$ must be applied explicitly; substituting gives $\frac{\sin^2\theta}{\sin^2\theta}\cdot\tan\theta = \tan\theta$. For part (ii), the equation factors as $(2\cos\theta + 1)(\cos\theta - 1) = 0$, giving $\cos\theta = -1/2$ or $\cos\theta = 1$; solutions: $\theta = 0, 2\pi/3, 4\pi/3$. All three required with no incorrect values. Note $\theta = 2\pi$ is not in $[0, 2\pi)$.

scoring_points.description: Correct simplification to $\tan\theta$; supporting work must show $1 - \cos^2\theta = \sin^2\theta$ being applied
wrong_examples: Correct identity but no simplification to a final answer
common_traps: The Pythagorean identity must appear explicitly — the shortcut $\frac{\sin^2}{\sin^2} = 1$ is only valid after showing $1 - \cos^2 = \sin^2$

scoring_points.description: All three correct solutions $\theta = 0$, $\theta = \frac{2\pi}{3}$, $\theta = \frac{4\pi}{3}$ from factoring the quadratic; no incorrect values
common_traps: $\cos\theta = 1$ gives $\theta = 0$ (and $2\pi$, but $2\pi$ is excluded from $[0, 2\pi)$) — do not forget this solution

parts.prompt:
(i) Evaluate $\sin\left(\arctan\left(\frac{3}{4}\right)\right)$ without a calculator. Express your answer as an exact fraction. Show supporting work.

(ii) The function $m$ is given by $m(x) = \arccos\left(\tan\left(\frac{x}{2}\right)\right)$. Find all values of $x$ in the interval $\left[0, \pi\right)$ for which $m(x) = \frac{\pi}{3}$. Show the computations that lead to your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: For part (i), right triangle with opposite $= 3$, adjacent $= 4$, hypotenuse $= \sqrt{9+16} = 5$; so $\sin(\arctan(3/4)) = 3/5$. For part (ii), $m(x) = \pi/3$ requires $\arccos(\tan(x/2)) = \pi/3$, so $\tan(x/2) = \cos(\pi/3) = 1/2$; then $x/2 = \arctan(1/2)$, giving $x = 2\arctan(1/2) \approx 0.927$ in $[0, \pi)$. Do not accept $x = \pi/3$ without the intermediate work. Supporting work required.

scoring_points.description: Correct exact value $\frac{3}{5}$; supporting work must show a right triangle with opposite $= 3$, adjacent $= 4$, hypotenuse $= 5$

alternatives.correct_example: Let $\phi = \arctan(3/4)$. Draw a right triangle with opposite $= 3$, adjacent $= 4$; hypotenuse $= \sqrt{9+16} = 5$. Therefore $\sin\phi = \frac{3}{5}$.

common_traps: $\arctan(3/4)$ is the angle, not the value — the triangle method is needed to find $\sin$ of that angle

scoring_points.description: Correct solution $x = 2\arctan(\frac{1}{2})$; supporting work must show $\tan(x/2) = \cos(\pi/3) = \frac{1}{2}$ as the key step
wrong_examples: $x = \pi/3$ without supporting work showing the composition was correctly unwrapped
$\tan(x/2) = \pi/3$ (using the output of $\arccos$ as its input)
common_traps: $m(x) = \pi/3$ does NOT mean $\tan(x/2) = \pi/3$ — the outer function is $\arccos$, so $\arccos(\tan(x/2)) = \pi/3$ means $\tan(x/2) = \cos(\pi/3)$
